title: EU Extends Myanmar Sanctions Until April 30, 2027 — 105 Individuals and 22 Entities Covered
detail: The European Union formally extended its sanctions framework against Myanmar on April 28, 2026, renewing restrictive measures against 105 individuals and 22 entities involved in the February 2021 military coup and subsequent human rights violations. The EU Council cited the 'continuing grave situation' in Myanmar, including systematic attacks on civilians, the use of military force against the population, and the ongoing detention of political prisoners including Aung San Suu Kyi. The sanctions — originally imposed in March 2021 — cover travel bans and asset freezes targeting senior military figures, commanders responsible for atrocities, and entities providing economic support to the junta. The renewal comes two days after China FM Wang Yi concluded his Naypyidaw visit pledging to 'firmly support Myanmar's sovereignty' — placing the EU and China in direct diplomatic opposition over Myanmar's trajectory. The EU's continued sanctions signal that major democratic powers do not recognize the legitimacy of the junta's 'civilian rule' transition under Min Aung Hlaing. The EU also renewed calls for the immediate and unconditional release of Aung San Suu Kyi and all political prisoners, rejecting the April 2026 Thingyan amnesty that nominally reduced her sentence but did not result in her release.

media.caption: EU extends Myanmar sanctions until April 30, 2027, covering 105 individuals and 22 entities over the coup and ongoing atrocities

title: PDF and Pa Ka Pha Forces Capture Aung Chan Thar Base in Kalewa Township — Ye-U–Kalewa Road Reopened
detail: Resistance forces composed of the People's Defence Force (PDF) and Pa Ka Pha units captured the Aung Chan Thar base in Kalewa Township, Sagaing Region, by April 28, 2026, in a significant multi-day offensive along the Chindwin River corridor. Junta troops retreated to Sin Ain Ma village following the fall of the base. The Ye-U–Kalewa road — a strategically important supply and logistics route in northwest Sagaing Region — was reopened following the operation, cutting junta overland resupply capacity to remaining SAC garrisons along the Chindwin. Kalewa is a town on the Chindwin River in western Sagaing Region near the Indian border, and its garrison has been under sustained resistance pressure for months. The capture of the Aung Chan Thar base is part of the broader PDF expansion in Sagaing Region that has, by ACLED estimates, left the junta in control of fewer than 100 of 330+ townships. MoeMaKa's April 28 CDM News report documented the operation as part of a broader list of resistance operations completed in the April 25–28 timeframe, reflecting continued offensive momentum by resistance forces across multiple fronts despite the SAC's April 24 martial law expansion across 60 townships.

media.caption: PDF and Pa Ka Pha forces capture Aung Chan Thar base in Kalewa Township, Sagaing Region — Ye-U–Kalewa road reopened after operation; junta troops retreat to Sin Ain Ma village

title: KNLA and Allied Forces Capture Kabawtu Base in Hpapun District — 68th Junta Position Seized in Mutraw Since 2021 Coup
detail: Karen National Liberation Army (KNLA) and allied resistance forces captured the Kabawtu military base in Hpapun District (Mutraw), Karen State, marking the 68th junta position seized in Mutraw District since the February 2021 coup. The resistance forces seized between 15 and 20 small arms, two machine guns, one mortar, landmines, grenades, and RPG rounds during the operation. The capture of Kabawtu continues the KNLA's systematic campaign to eliminate junta outposts in the deep-jungle terrain of Mutraw (Papun) District, one of the most heavily contested areas of Karen State and home to the Karen National Union's strongest administrative zone (Kawthoolei). Hpapun District has been the site of some of the most intense KNU/KNLA operations since the coup, with the resistance successfully dismantling the vast majority of the junta's outpost network in the district over 5+ years of fighting. The 68-position milestone since 2021 reflects the KNLA's methodical clearance of junta presence from Mutraw territory — transforming it into one of the most comprehensively resistance-controlled districts in Myanmar. The operation occurred in the same geographic zone as the April 15, 2026 SAC airstrike on Dae Bu Noh junior college that killed 4 civilians.

media.caption: KNLA and allied forces capture Kabawtu base in Hpapun District — 68th junta position seized in Mutraw (Papun) District since the 2021 coup; 15-20 small arms and heavy weapons seized
